label: Squarish 1BR / 1 bath, LD style — PROOF-OF-CONCEPT sibling of 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr: same private column (bedroom front / bath rear, left), but the public column splits into separate living / dining / kitchen (stacked front-to-rear) instead of one great_room + kitchen.

adjacencies.note: bedroom (front) and bath (rear) stack vertically along the left wall -- shared wall only, no door between them, same as the gr sibling.
bedroom's only door opens east into living -- the arrival room, same role great_room played in the gr sibling.
bath's only door opens onto kitchen, same as the gr sibling -- both are rear_anchored so they align at the rear wall regardless of the public column now stacking 3 rooms instead of 2.
living and dining flow as one open public space -- no wall (ld style, matches the working 2BR _ld precedent).
dining flows into kitchen -- open pass-through (PH practice, matches the working 2BR _ld precedent).

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_side_split_bath_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: PROOF-OF-CONCEPT: copied as-is from the gr sibling's compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr profile (same room types, same reasoning) pending real feasibility testing. Not yet tuned for the living/dining split specifically -- expect this to need adjustment once a real sweep is run.

notes: PROOF-OF-CONCEPT topology -- created 2026-07-20 to answer a feasibility question
(does a true living/dining/kitchen split work for a squarish 1BR program, or is
the counter_divider great_room approach the only thing that fits). NOT yet
swept for min/med/max, NOT yet added to briefs/test/, NOT yet in the published
artol-topologies catalog or CLAUDE.md. If it proves out, treat this file as a
draft to be tuned (lot_adjustment_profiles especially) before it graduates to a
Direct sibling of 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr: identical private column
(bedroom front-left / bath rear-left). The public column swaps great_room +
kitchen (2-room open-plan) for living + dining + kitchen (3-room stack,
living front / dining mid / kitchen rear), following the same wiring pattern
are NOT width-matched to each other (per that precedent, which lets the
public column's rooms vary in width rather than over-constraining a 3-way
match) -- only right_anchored on kitchen, not living/dining, for the same
over-constrained-anchors reason documented across this catalog's 3BR fixes.
